name: Sweet E's Café
tagline: Kapahulu brunch institution with legendary French toast flights and loco moco — where locals actually eat breakfast.
mustOrder: French toast flight — try multiple flavors in one sitting
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave; arrive early on weekends or you'll circle
bestTime: Weekdays before 9am to avoid the wait. Weekend lines can be 30–45 min by 9:30am
talkStory: Sweet E's has been the Kapahulu brunch spot since opening, earning a devoted local following that rivals the more Instagrammed places. The French toast flight lets you try multiple signature flavors — crème brûlée, Fruity Pebbles, Oreo — without committing to one. The loco moco is a proper rendition with thick homemade gravy. The space is cozy (read: small), which is why the wait gets real on weekends. Not a tourist brunch spot — you'll be surrounded by local families and regulars. The Kapahulu corridor location puts you walking distance from Diamond Head and the zoo.
